## Summary

<!-- What problem does this solve, and why is this approach appropriate? -->

## Release impact

<!-- The PR title must be a Conventional Commit and should match the consumer impact. -->

- [ ] No package release (`docs`, `test`, `refactor`, `build`, `ci`, or `chore`)
- [ ] Patch (`fix` or `perf`)
- [ ] Minor (`feat`)
- [ ] Major (`!` and a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer)

## Validation

- [ ] Added or updated tests for observable behavior
- [ ] Ran `npm run validate`
- [ ] Checked accessibility, SSR/hydration, zoneless, and RTL impact where relevant
- [ ] Updated README, changelog, and migration guidance where relevant
- [ ] Verified that no credentials, generated output, or unrelated changes are included

## Visual changes
